channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Alphabet’s Waymo will expand its robotaxi fleet with Hyundai EVs
item.description: Alphabet’s Waymo will add electric vehicles from South Korea’s Hyundai Motor to its robotaxi fleet, the companies said on Friday, as Waymo expands in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Civilization 7 makers work with Shawnee to bring sincere representation of the tribe to the game
item.description: MIAMI, Okla. (AP) — Shawnee Tribe Chief Ben Barnes grew up playing video games, including “probably hundreds of hours” colonizing a distant planet in the 1999 title Sid Meier's Alpha Centauri.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How many jobs are available in technology in the US?
item.description: Unemployment data released today showed surprising growth overall for technology job listings and hiring, but also marked shift in the kind of workers organizations need — with AI talent no longer at the top.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: We’ve entered the AI grift era
item.description: For years, Y Combinator has been revered as a highly selective startup accelerator with a hit rate that far exceeds the law of averages. But one of its latest companies has raised a few eyebrows, and prompted questions about whether AI grifters have caught up with the innovators.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Apple, Amkor, and TSMC ‘neath the Arizona Skies
item.description: It is strategically essential for the US to bring home the manufacture of key components for the technology used across government, consumer, and enterprise markets, an imperative impacting IT leadership that will generate change across the coming decade.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Netflix just canceled That '90s Show but there’s a better-rated series with 96% on Rotten Tomatoes being added in October
item.description: Netflix cancels That '90s Show, but there's an even better series being added that you can watch next.
item.dc:creator: grace.morris@futurenet.com (Grace Morris), TechRadar

item.title: Scammers are flooding Trump’s new crypto exchange
item.description: Back in September, Donald Trump announced his new crypto exchange, World Liberty Financial (WLFI). Now, registered investors can officially sign up for the whitelist—assuming they can avoid the scammers.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Open AI is testing a new interface for ChatGPT — ‘Canvas’
item.description: Open AI has unveiled Canvas, a new ChatGPT interface specifically crafted for developers that is now available in beta. Canvas has been developed using GPT-4o and makes it possible, among other things, to use a separate window for code.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft promises Windows 11’s Edge browser will become “very fast” – but will it be enough to beat Chrome?
item.description: From December, Microsoft Edge’s settings page is going to be more responsive. It’s the latest of Edge’s components ported by Microsoft to its new WebUI 2.0 toolkit.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Musk to attend Trump rally at site of attempted assassination
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said he plans to attend former President Trump’s rally in battleground Pennsylvania this weekend, when Trump returns to the site where he survived the first assassination attempt on his life.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Cyber Leaders Exchange: Army, SSA and USCIS cyber chiefs on securing software through automation
item.description: Cybersecurity experts from the Army Software Factory, SSA and USCIS say letting developers drive security efforts drives up safety in code. The post Cyber Leaders Exchange: Army, SSA and USCIS cyber chiefs on securing software through automation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Understanding exit codes on Linux
item.description: Whenever you run a command in a Linux terminal, a numeric exit code is generated – even if you see no sign of it. The code reports the success or failure of the command. And, if the command fails, it provides a value that hints at the problem. If the exit code is 0, all is well.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: De la ‘sala de máquinas’ a la junta directiva: el nuevo rol del CIO, a debate en el CIO Summit 2024
item.description: Los debates marcaron, una vez más, gran parte de la agenda de la última edición de CIO Summit 2024, celebrado en Madrid los pasados 25 y 26 de septiembre.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Mystery surge in California tax revenue points to tech companies like Nvidia
item.description: California was nearly $2 billion over forecast in corporate tax receipts this summer, including by $844 million in July.
item.dc:creator: CalMatters, The Mercury News

item.title: Austrian activist Schrems wins privacy case against Meta over personal data on sexual orientation
item.description: The European Union’s top court has said that social media company Meta can’t use public information about a user’s sexual orientation obtained outside its platforms for personalized advertising under the bloc’s strict data privacy rules.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Google says it will stop linking to New Zealand news if a law passes forcing it to pay for content
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — Google said Friday it will stop linking to New Zealand news content and will reverse its support of local media outlets if the government passes a law forcing tech companies to pay for articles displayed on their platforms.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Revolutionizing Home Life with NEO Beta: The Humanoid Robot Companion
item.description: Discover the innovative features and potential impact of NEO Beta, a humanoid robot designed for home environments, from 1X Technologies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What is RPA? A revolution in business process automation
item.description: What is robotic process automation? Robotic process automation (RPA) is an application of technology, governed by business logic and structured inputs, aimed at automating business processes.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Things get nasty in lawsuit between WordPress.org and WP Engine
item.description: After years of nasty back and forth accusations between WordPress hosting provider WP Engine (WPE) and WordPress.org, the open source project that provides the software, on Wednesday WPE sued WordPress.org in federal court, accusing WordPress.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How to use extension methods in C#
item.description: In the C# programming language, extension methods enable you to extend the functionality of existing types without modifying them or deriving new types from them using inheritance.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Understanding VBS Enclaves, Windows’ new security technology
item.description: Recently Microsoft announced efforts to improve security in its planned Recall AI assistant. Many of the details were not a surprise, as it built on familiar tools and services I’ve written about during the past few years, but the most important feature, VBS Enclaves, will be new to most of us.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI previews Realtime API for speech-to-speech apps
item.description: OpenAI has introduced a public beta of the Realtime API, an API that allows paid developers to build low-latency, multi-modal experiences including text and speech in apps.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: DoJ revising vulnerability disclosure framework to encourage AI red teaming
item.description: DoJ says its policy will encourage independent security and safety research, but will large AI companies follow suit in encouraging vulnerability disclosure? The post DoJ revising vulnerability disclosure framework to encourage AI red teaming first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: ChatGPT maker OpenAI raises $6.6 billion in fresh funding as it moves away from its nonprofit roots
item.description: OpenAI says it has raised $6.6 billion in venture capital investments as part of a broader shift by the ChatGPT maker away from its nonprofit roots.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Women of color running for Congress disproportionately targeted by offensive speech on X: Report
item.description: Congressional candidates who are women, specifically those of color, are more likely than other candidates to be targeted by offensive or hate speech on the social platform X, according to a new study.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: IBM Unveils European Quantum Data Center, Promising New Opportunities and Solutions
item.description: IBM's new data center in Ehningen, Germany will serve as a European cloud region for IBM Quantum, offering access to state-of-the-art quantum computers and promoting the development of talent in this area across Europe.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Tesla posts first quarterly increase in deliveries, but shares slump with investors hoping for more
item.description: Low interest financing, sweet lease deals, price cuts and free charging boosted Tesla’s global deliveries in the third quarter, the first increase this year for the electric vehicle maker.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DoD agencies confront zero trust challenges, misunderstandings ahead of 2026 deadline
item.description: The Defense Department’s zero trust framework is acting as a catalyst, driving mission owners to industry in search of zero trust solutions The post DoD agencies confront zero trust challenges, misunderstandings ahead of 2026 deadline first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Docker tutorial: Get started with Docker
item.description: Containers are a lightweight way to make application workloads portable, like a virtual machine but without the overhead and bulk typically associated with VMs. With containers, you can package apps and services and move them freely between physical, virtual, or cloud environments.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft releases official OpenAI library for .NET
item.description: Microsoft has released its official OpenAI library for .NET, with the goal of ensuring a smooth, reliable integration experience for developers working with OpenAI and Azure OpenAI services in .NET applications. Announced October 1, the now-stable library follows a beta release published in June.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Inside HHS’ ‘one-stop shop’ for health sector cybersecurity
item.description: In the wake of the Change Healthcare ransomware attack, HHS has been expanding its "one-stop shop" for working with the healthcare sector on cyber issues. The post Inside HHS’ ‘one-stop shop’ for health sector cybersecurity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: For Fiscal Service, intelligent automation transforms federal workflows
item.description: At the Bureau of the Fiscal Service, automation is helping to reduce manual workloads, and to increase employee efficiency and morale. The post For Fiscal Service, intelligent automation transforms federal workflows first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Dana Sukontarak, Federal News Network

item.title: Details from New Mexico's lawsuit against Snap show site failed to act on reports of sextortion
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico's attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Intel launches Xeon 6 processors and Gaudi 3 AI accelerators
item.description: Intel has formally launched its next Xeon 6 server processors as well as the Gaudi 3 AI accelerators, making some pretty big boasts in the process.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Think twice before using public computers: Here's why
item.description: Using public computers poses security risks, tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says, so take precautions or use your own secure device.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to succeed with Kubernetes
item.description: Kubernetes, the open source orchestration platform for automating the deployment and management of containerized workloads and services, continues to gain popularity.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cruise ship surgery that slices giants for mega profits
item.description: A whopping 30 million people will have taken cruises in 2024, and some ships being built to keep up with this demand cost as much as $2 billion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
